question: Panthers vs. Penguins
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 4 at 5:00PM ET:
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the Penguins win, the market will resolve to "Penguins".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Pittsburgh Penguins, driven by their elite 10-2-1 Atlantic Division lead and six-game win streak fueled by Sergei Bobrovsky's .929 save percentage. Pittsburgh sits at 6-7-2 amid defensive woes, allowing 3.7 goals per game, exacerbated by Sidney Crosby's recent minus-5 rating over four contests. No major injuries reported on official updates—Matthew Tkachuk and Aleksander Barkov are good to go for Florida—while Penguins' Kris Letang returns from maintenance. Home-ice advantage at PPG Paints Arena and a rested Penguins squad after a day off could spark an upset, but Panthers' road dominance (5-1-1) tempers expectations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Winnipeg Jets hold a commanding edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Columbus Blue Jackets, driven by the Jets' elite 6-2-1 start fueled by Connor Hellebuyck's .941 save percentage and stingy team defense allowing just 2.1 goals per game. Recent Jets momentum includes a five-game point streak, while the Blue Jackets dropped three straight road contests. Home-ice advantage at Canada Life Centre and Winnipeg's superior 4-1 home record further tilt implied probabilities, though Columbus' desperation for a win could spark an upset in this Central Division clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Washington Capitals enter as trader favorites against the Buffalo Sabres, reflecting their superior recent form with five wins in seven games and elite goaltending from Charlie Lindgren. Key development: Tage Thompson's confirmed upper-body injury sidelines Buffalo's top scorer, per official report, forcing reliance on Alex Tuch and JJ Peterka amid a middling 3-4-1 road record. Caps gain edge from Alex Ovechkin's goal chase and Tom Wilson's return from suspension, enhancing forecheck. Sabres boast Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's .915 save percentage but face rest disadvantage after back-to-back. Historical splits favor Washington 6-4 in last 10 meetings, underscoring defensive matchup as pivotal for outcome probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vancouver Canucks enter their home matchup against the Utah Hockey Club as clear trader favorites, bolstered by a hot streak of three straight wins and elite goaltending from Thatcher Demko, who's posted a .945 save percentage in October. Utah, still gelling as a relocated expansion squad, has struggled defensively, allowing 4.2 goals per game amid injuries to forwards Clayton Keller (day-to-day, upper body) and Logan Cooley (questionable). Canucks' potent power play (28.6% efficiency) exploits Utah's league-worst penalty kill (71.4%), while Vancouver's rest advantage after a lighter schedule sharpens their edge. Historical head-to-heads favor Vancouver 4-1-1 since Utah's franchise shift, underscoring trader sentiment implied in current odds.

question: Utah vs. Canucks: O/U 4.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 4 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Utah and Canucks combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Jersey Devils hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Montreal Canadiens, with implied probabilities reflecting the Devils' superior 9-4-1 record and potent offense led by Jack Hughes (1.3 points per game). Montreal sits at 5-7-1, hampered by defensive lapses allowing 3.5 goals per game lately. Key recent development: Canadiens' goaltender Samuel Montembeault is probable after missing time, but backup Cayden Primeau's .878 save percentage underscores netminding concerns; Devils' Jacob Markstrom boasts a .915 SV% in recent starts. Upcoming factors include Devils' home-ice advantage at Prudential Center and Montreal's road fatigue from a three-game trip, tilting momentum toward New Jersey amid their five wins in seven.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kings' home-ice edge and goaltending tandem of Talbot and Copley anchor trader consensus favoring Los Angeles at around 60% implied probability, buoyed by their 5-2-1 Pacific Division start despite a recent two-game skid. Toronto's high-octane attack led by Auston Matthews (10 goals) counters with a four-game win streak, but forward Matthew Knies remains sidelined (upper-body, per official report), thinning depth scoring amid a road-heavy schedule. Kings' Anze Kopitar dominates faceoffs (58% win rate), while Leafs' penalty kill (89%) exploits LA's middling power play. Momentum tilts Toronto, yet historical Kings' home dominance (6-3-1 last 10 vs. Leafs) sustains even odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes lead the Eastern Conference Second Round series 2-1 against the New York Islanders after a 5-3 road win in Game 3, fueling trader consensus toward Carolina at roughly 70% implied probability for advancement. Key driver is Frederik Andersen's elite goaltending (.932 save percentage through three games) versus Ilya Sorokin's struggles (.905), compounded by Islanders' defensive injuries sidelining Casey Cizikas and defenseman Adam Pelech. Recent Hurricanes momentum from depth scoring (11 different goal scorers) contrasts Islanders' reliance on Bo Horvat and Mathew Barzal. Game 4 in Raleigh leverages Carolina's 8-2 home playoff record, though New York's desperation could spark an upset akin to their Game 2 comeback.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes lead the Eastern Conference Second Round series 2-1 against the New York Islanders after a 5-3 road win in Game 3, fueling trader consensus toward Carolina at roughly 70% implied probability for advancement. Key driver is Frederik Andersen's elite goaltending (.932 save percentage through three games) versus Ilya Sorokin's struggles (.905), compounded by Islanders' defensive injuries sidelining Casey Cizikas and defenseman Adam Pelech. Recent Hurricanes momentum from depth scoring (11 different goal scorers) contrasts Islanders' reliance on Bo Horvat and Mathew Barzal. Game 4 in Raleigh leverages Carolina's 8-2 home playoff record, though New York's desperation could spark an upset akin to their Game 2 comeback.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Kraken hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their home matchup against the Chicago Blackhawks, reflecting the Hawks' ongoing rebuild and poor early-season form (1-3-1 record) versus Kraken's solid 2-2-0 start with strong defensive play. Critical factor: Blackhawks' injury woes, including top prospect Frank Nazar out with an upper-body issue and Seth Jones questionable with a foot injury, thinning their blue line against Kraken's potent forecheck led by Jared McCann (4 goals). Kraken goalie Joey Daccord's recent shutout bolsters their implied probability, while Chicago's road struggles (0-2) and rest disadvantage after a back-to-back amplify upset risk. Watch pre-game lineups for Bedard-Jones pairing impact.

eventMetadata.context_description: Calgary Flames hold the edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the Anaheim Ducks, driven by a stronger 3-2-0 start compared to Anaheim's 1-3-1 record amid a three-game skid. Flames' goaltender Dustin Wolf has posted a .930 save percentage in recent outings, bolstering their defensive edge, while Ducks face uncertainty with forward Trevor Zegras sidelined by ankle surgery recovery and goaltender John Gibson day-to-day with an injury. Calgary's road form remains solid (2-1-0 away), and their power play ranks top-10 league-wide. Anaheim's home-ice advantage at Honda Center could spark a rally, but Flames' momentum and rest edge (extra day off) tilt probabilities in their favor per market pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nashville Predators enter their matchup against the San Jose Sharks as heavy favorites, reflecting trader consensus on the Preds' explosive 5-2-0 start fueled by Filip Forsberg's scoring surge and stout defense allowing just 2.3 goals per game, contrasted with the Sharks' dismal 0-5-1 record marred by goaltending woes and bottom-five power play. Recent developments include Predators winger Gustav Nyquist returning from minor injury for added depth, while Sharks face absences of key forwards like Tomas Hertl (lower body) per official reports, widening the talent gap. Upcoming factors: Preds' home-ice edge at Bridgestone Arena and rest advantage after a lighter schedule could amplify momentum, though Sharks' desperation in a rebuild year invites upset potential as seen in early-season volatility.

Penguins face rest disadvantages following a back-to-back, while Florida's home-ice at Amerant Arena adds momentum—watch for special teams edges, where Panthers rank top-5 on the power play.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes enter as heavy favorites against the Ottawa Senators, driven by their league-leading 7-1-0 start and elite defensive metrics allowing just 1.6 goals per game. Recent 4-1 rout of New Jersey underscores momentum from Frederik Andersen's .941 save percentage, while Ottawa's Linus Ullmark (2.03 GAA) provides stability in net but faces a Hurricanes attack averaging 4.25 goals. Senators' 3-3-2 record shows resilience post-playoff miss, yet road woes (1-2-1) and injuries to Josh Norris loom large. Home-ice edge and rest advantage tilt probabilities toward Carolina, reflecting trader consensus on the Hurricanes' superior form and matchup history (5-1-0 last six meetings).

Washington’s Alex Ovechkin nears Gretzky’s goal record with 40 tallies, but the Caps face lineup questions after Tom Wilson’s three-game suspension for a high hit, thinning their physical edge in this heated rivalry. Recent form shows Rangers winning four of five, bolstered by Artemi Panarin’s 100-point pace, while Caps split last six amid road woes; watch for Charlie Lindgren’s bounce-back in net amid playoff positioning stakes.

question: Claude 4.7 released by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Anthropic makes any Claude 4.7 model available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Claude 4.7 refers to a product explicitly named Claude Opus, Sonnet, or Haiku 4.7, or a variant that is recognized as a successor to Claude 4.6, similar to the progression from Claude 4.2 to Claude 4.3. (e.g., Claude Opus, Sonnet, or Haiku 4.8, 4.9, etc., would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market)

Qualifying releases of task-specialized models, cost-efficiency variants, or similar will count for this market. 

Products labeled as a new flagship generation Claude 5 or similar will NOT qualify.

For this market to resolve to "Yes," a qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. 

The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by Anthropic as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Anthropic, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader skepticism around a Claude 4.7 release stems primarily from Anthropic's absence of any official roadmap signaling a jump beyond the Claude 3.5 Sonnet model, launched in June 2024 as its current flagship with superior coding and reasoning benchmarks over rivals like GPT-4o. Recent developments, including the rollout of "Artifacts" for interactive AI outputs and experimental "computer use" capabilities, indicate iterative enhancements to Claude 3.x rather than a major version leap. Competitive pressure mounts from OpenAI's o1 reasoning series and Google's Gemini 2.0 rumors, yet Anthropic's safety-first ethos prioritizes refinements over rushed versioning. Watch for hints at AWS re:Invent (November 2024) or investor updates, as delays in frontier models are common amid scaling challenges.

events.title: Nagoya Grampus vs. Cerezo Ōsaka - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J1 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 4 at 2:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cerezo Osaka's strong recent form—four wins in their last six J.League matches—and fifth-place standing fuel trader consensus favoring them over Nagoya Grampus, who sit 10th amid a mixed run of two wins in five. Nagoya's captain Sho Inagaki is confirmed out with a hamstring injury, weakening their midfield control, while Cerezo boasts a near-full squad including in-form striker Thiago Andrade. Nagoya's solid home record at Toyota Stadium (unbeaten in last five) and a 1-0 win over Cerezo in May provide counterbalance, but Cerezo's momentum and head-to-head edge in goals scored heighten upset potential for this October 19 clash with playoff implications looming.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $70 by end of March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price between market creation and the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a subdued 35% implied probability for WTI crude oil (CL) surpassing $85 by March 31, anchored by persistent OPEC+ supply discipline offsetting tepid global demand amid China's uneven recovery and U.S. recession fears. Front-month futures hover near $78/bbl after last week's EIA report revealed a 3.4 million barrel inventory build, exceeding expectations and capping upside momentum. Geopolitical risks from Red Sea disruptions provide tailwind, but softening U.S. economic data weighs heavy. Traders eye Thursday's EIA storage update and next week's OPEC+ committee meeting as pivotal catalysts that could shift market-implied odds decisively.

question: T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for March 15 2026 in T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - Most Sixes Afghanistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-15 in T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Afghanistan will be considered correct if Afghanistan is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sri Lanka.The outcome corresponding to Sri Lanka will be considered correct if Sri Lanka is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Afghanistan.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Afghanistan holds a slim edge in the most sixes market at 52.0% implied probability, but razor-thin margins to Sri Lanka (51.0%) and draw (51.5%) underscore balanced power-hitting threats in this T20I. Afghanistan's Rahmanullah Gurbaz, fresh off a six-studded IPL stint, pairs with Azmatullah Omarzai's middle-overs muscle, fueling trader optimism amid their recent momentum in short formats. Sri Lanka counters via Kusal Perera's explosive opening and Charith Asalanka's finishing flair, bolstered by Dambulla's compact boundaries favoring big hits. No key injuries reported, yet variable weather risks a truncated contest tilting toward draw bets, keeping the wisdom of crowds tightly clustered.

question: T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - Team Top Batter Afghanistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-15 in T20 Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Afghanistan will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Afghanistan.
The outcome corresponding to Sri Lanka will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Sri Lanka.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering of Draw (51%), Sri Lanka (50%), and Afghanistan (48.5%) in the Team Top Batter market reflects evenly matched top-order firepower ahead of the Dambulla T20I opener. question: Will 20 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day in March?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above __ for any date between market creation and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The number of daily transit calls/arrivals includes container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points, made within this market’s timeframe, will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for March 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

question: Will CF Monterrey win on 2026-04-04?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 4, 2026
If CF Monterrey wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CF Monterrey vs. Atlético San Luis
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Liga MX game, scheduled for Saturday, April 4, 2026 between CF Monterrey and Atlético San Luis.

eventMetadata.context_description: CF Monterrey's home dominance at Estadio BBVA clashes with Atlético San Luis's resilient road form, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus where Monterrey and draw odds sit at 48.5% apiece ahead of San Luis at 43.5%. Recent developments amplify the uncertainty: Monterrey enters unbeaten in five straight Liga MX home matches but has drawn three of their last four overall, hampered by key midfielder Celso Ortiz nursing a minor knock per official reports. San Luis, meanwhile, boasts a stingy defense conceding just 0.8 goals per away game lately and stunned Tigres 2-1 last outing, showcasing momentum under coach Gustavo Leal. Head-to-head history favors Rayados (4 wins in last 5), but San Luis's counterattacking threat and Monterrey's fatigue from midweek CONCACAF action keep probabilities tightly contested, reflecting crowd wisdom on a potential stalemate.
